audienceSections.normal: In Layla and Sami’s café plan, you will use [[grammar:أُرِيدُ + N]]. Use [[grammar:أُرِيدُ]] before the thing you want. In أُرِيدُ قَهْوَةً, the speaker wants coffee. Do not use the third-person verb form when you mean ‘I want.’
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:أُرِيدُ + N]] in Layla and Sami’s café plan. أُرِيدُ is a first-person singular imperfect transitive verb with an accusative noun-phrase complement. وَأَنَا adds a coordinated topic, while نَذْهَبُ is a separately inflected occurrence of the already tracked verb ذَهَبَ.

audienceSections.normal: Use [[grammar:أُرِيدُ]] before the thing you want. In أُرِيدُ قَهْوَةً, the speaker wants coffee. Do not use the third-person verb form when you mean ‘I want.’
audienceSections.expert: أُرِيدُ is a first-person singular imperfect transitive verb with an accusative noun-phrase complement. وَأَنَا adds a coordinated topic, while نَذْهَبُ is a separately inflected occurrence of the already tracked verb ذَهَبَ.

audienceSections.normal: The Arabic lines are in Modern Standard Arabic. The vowel marks show short vowels and case endings, so read every marked vowel instead of guessing from the bare consonants.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses fully vocalized Modern Standard Arabic. Lexical citation forms follow the established perfect-stem convention for verbs, while the learner text preserves the attested inflected form and case vocalization.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact arabic line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:أُرِيدُ + N]] where it appears.

- أُرِيدُ قَهْوَةً. — I want coffee.
- وَأَنَا أُرِيدُ شَايًا. — And I want tea.
- هَلْ نَذْهَبُ إِلَى الْمَقْهَى؟ — Shall we go to the café?
- نَعَمْ، نَذْهَبُ الْيَوْمَ. — Yes, we are going today.
- الْمَقْهَى قُرْبَ الْبَيْتِ. — The café is near the house.
- جَيِّدٌ، هَيَّا بِنَا. — Good, let's go.
breakdown.expert:
These exact reading lines provide the distributional evidence for [[grammar:أُرِيدُ + N]]. The grammar section above analyzes the relevant morphology and syntax.
